tasks.title: Verify ball speed indicator implementation
tasks.description: The ball speed indicator feature is already fully implemented in the codebase. Verify that all acceptance criteria are met by reading the relevant code in index.html and js/main.js. If any criteria is not fully met, make the minimal fix. The speed element (#speed) already exists in the HTML with correct styling, and the game loop in main.js already computes Math.sqrt(vx² + vz²), formats it to 1 decimal place with 'm/s' label, and shows/hides it appropriately.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: 1. #speed element visible in lower-left corner during gameplay (bottom:16px, left:16px). 2. Speed updates every frame from Math.sqrt(vx²+vz²). 3. Displayed as X.X m/s (toFixed(1)). 4. Hidden on start/game-over, visible during active play. 5. Visual style matches #score and #level HUD elements.
tasks.claudeMd:
# Project Context

You are verifying an already-implemented ball speed indicator in a vanilla JS browser game.

## Tech Stack
- Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, no build system, no package.json
- Three.js v0.183.2 via CDN import map
- Static files served by nginx (Dockerfile)
- No test framework

## Key Files
- `index.html` — contains `#speed` element (lines 283-298) and its DOM node (line 323)
- `js/main.js` — game loop with speed calculation (lines 344-345), show/hide logic (lines 188, 223, 292)
- `js/physics.js` — returns `vx` and `vz` in physics result object

## What To Do
1. Read `index.html` and `js/main.js` to confirm all 5 acceptance criteria are met
2. The feature is already implemented — only make changes if something is actually missing or broken
3. If everything looks correct, commit with message `chore: verify speed indicator implementation`
4. Do NOT introduce new dependencies, frameworks, or restructure existing code

## Conventions
- Match existing code style: no semicolons inconsistency (the codebase uses semicolons), 2-space indentation
- HUD elements use: `-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif`, white color, `font-weight: 700`, `rgba(0,0,0,0.3)` background, `border-radius: 8px`

checklist.description: Speed element exists in HTML with correct positioning (bottom:16px, left:16px)
steps: Read index.html
Verify #speed CSS has position:fixed, bottom:16px, left:16px
Verify <div id='speed'> exists in body

checklist.description: Speed is computed each frame as Math.sqrt(vx²+vz²) and displayed with toFixed(1) + ' m/s'
steps: Read js/main.js
Verify gameLoop contains speed calculation from result.vx and result.vz
Verify speedEl.textContent is set to speed.toFixed(1) + ' m/s'

checklist.description: Speed indicator hidden on start/game-over, visible during active play
steps: Verify #speed has display:none in CSS (hidden by default on page load)
Verify speedEl.style.display = 'block' in init() when game starts
Verify speedEl.style.display = 'none' in enterGameOver()
Verify speedEl.style.display = 'block' in exitGameOver()

checklist.description: Visual style matches existing HUD elements (score, level)
steps: Compare #speed CSS with #score and #level CSS
Verify same font-family, color, font-weight, text-shadow, background, border-radius, padding

quality_checklist.description: No unnecessary code duplication or dead code introduced
steps: Check that speed element is only referenced where needed
Verify no duplicate speed calculations exist

checks.description: Speed indicator element exists and is styled correctly in the DOM
steps: Start the static file server
Open the page in browser
Verify #speed element is present in the DOM
Verify it is initially hidden (display:none)

checks.description: Speed indicator becomes visible and updates during gameplay
steps: Allow camera access and wait for game to start
Verify #speed element becomes visible (display:block)
Verify speed value updates each frame showing format like '4.5 m/s'
